text:
AI Interviewer ROI and Key Metrics to Track

WeCP's guide examines how organizations should measure the actual return on investment from AI interviewing platforms.

Key Metrics Highlighted:
1. Time-to-Hire Reduction - tracking how much faster candidates move through screening
2. Candidate Drop-Off Rate - monitoring completion rates and user experience issues
3. Quality of Hire - measuring post-hire performance, retention, and manager satisfaction
4. Interview Score Alignment - comparing AI-generated scores against human evaluator assessments
5. Bias Reduction Index - analyzing score disparities across demographic groups
6. Candidate Experience Score - gathering satisfaction ratings and Net Promoter Scores

ROI Calculation Framework: subtract platform costs from time and cost savings, then divide by platform costs and multiply by 100. A concrete example shows '10,00,000 in annual recruiter hour savings minus 3,00,000 tool cost yields 233% ROI.'

Common Implementation Errors: Organizations frequently stumble by relying on 'surface-level stats' rather than outcome-based KPIs, neglecting to validate AI scores against human judgment, and overlooking customization needs for different job roles.

WeCP's AI interviewer is positioned as supporting integrated workflows, compliance monitoring, and customizable role-specific assessments.
